name: video-replication
purpose: Provide the craft method for source-story video replication, image replacement, compact Pre-Seedance pack assembly, Seedance generation, and fast final QC.
output_contract: Story analysis comes before voiceover or Seedance prompts.
Image stages produce saved current-job AI-edited storyboard assets, not source frames or composites.
Matpool GPT-Image-2 work records edit/reference contract evidence when used.
Visible product text declared by product profiles remains readable in product close-ups.
Multi-person storyboards use a role map so the approved identity applies only to the protagonist/product-host role.
New director plans lock evidence-confirmed source dialogue and allow only declared localized line edits.

Version-6 source-length plans preserve every source beat exactly once and require declared necessary-only visual edits while keeping shot order, scene, camera, framing, action stage/timing, and hard cuts locked.
When no model assets are supplied for a multi-person source, approved storyboards may derive role-bound photoreal identities with current-job provenance and per-Part upload mappings.
After image batch PASS, the default path builds one Pre-Seedance pack instead of serial voiceover, seam, Seedance prompt, audio boundary, and request stages.
Final Seedance prompts are model-facing production briefs without loop-only labels.
Final prompt reference roles use definition, lock/control, and exclusion statements instead of free-form calibration wording.
Final Seedance prompts are derived from story analysis, ASR/subtitles, visual timeline, product profile, voiceover, seam notes, and approved references through a shot-line map.
Final Seedance prompts distinguish narration from in-frame synchronous speech and prevent product-reference packshot leakage by binding product refs to identity/material only.
Execution blocks split at scene, visual-function, or spoken speaker-mode boundaries; short still-life B-roll is standalone.
Prompt-only repair changes one declared failing time-and-Shot block; accepted blocks, assets, and Parts remain locked.
Physical-change Shots state contact, visible motion, and resulting state instead of relying on an abstract action verb.
Identity references do not transfer background or override Shot-specific skin state.
Profile-specific commercial effect requirements are not weakened by generic realism clauses.
Multi-Part final assembly concatenates only accepted Part outputs and preserves their actual submitted prompts and requests as evidence.
Artifact hashes remain internal stale-evidence, QC-reuse, and provenance fingerprints; they are not generation seeds or user workflow steps.

Shot-label normalization runs before the first content review; proven label-bar-only changes reuse content QC by panel-content fingerprint instead of triggering image generation or broad visual revalidation.
Final delivery QC is a fast technical check and does not run final ASR unless audio/script verification is explicitly requested or an audio defect is being debugged.
Skincare and mud-mask work preserves source rhythm while enforcing before/after and white-mud rules.
exclusions: Does not own jobs.csv selection, cost approval, or STATE.md writeback.
Does not bypass viral-replica loop gates when running inside viral-replica-loop.
Does not package deprecated or failed visual candidates as upload assets.
rollback_boundary: Fast default workflow changes may be reverted by restoring archive/20260703-pre-fast-default without modifying current queue state or deleting job artifacts; loop state rollback belongs to viral-replica.
